payload.title: Inbox submission must enforce target locus and cwd root authority
payload.summary: A User Site builder bypassed local architect routing and submitted an upstream proposal from the wrong cwd, producing a portable inbox envelope under packages/layers/cli/.ai instead of Narada proper root .ai. The content was coherent, but the route and locus were wrong.

incident.expected_route: builder -> User Site architect inbox -> architect review/routing -> Narada proper root inbox
incident.actual_route: builder -> Narada proper package cwd .ai inbox artifact

evidence: Root narada inbox show could not find env_b7d3aeae-cd59-4cc8-a323-140192da4194 after pulling the commit.
The artifact exists under packages/layers/cli/.ai/inbox-envelopes, outside Narada proper root inbox export_dir.
The User Site CAPA envelope env_537e5bb6-491c-4b23-a7d3-a65bd01e0bab documents the local architect-routing bypass.
Corrected root envelope env_b7330900-7040-4e9f-bb5f-93475bf24f28 supersedes the misplaced artifact for processing.
payload.proposed_direction: Inbox submit should preflight that --cwd resolves to the intended Site root and that target_locus is allowed for the principal/source Site/envelope kind. Submitting from package subdirectories should either resolve to the governing repo root or fail with an explicit repair command. Builder-facing defaults should submit to local architect, with direct upstream requiring explicit authority.
payload.requested_handling: Admit as corrective work adjacent to message routing authority, inbox publication ergonomics, and role-specific builder guardrails.
